endpoint.title: The host and port of GreptimeDB gRPC service.
endpoint.description:
This sink uses GreptimeDB's gRPC interface for data ingestion. By
default, GreptimeDB listens to port 4001 for gRPC protocol.

The address _must_ include a port.

grpc_compression.description:
Set gRPC compression encoding for the request
Default to none, `gzip` or `zstd` is supported.

new_naming.title: Use Greptime's prefixed naming for time index and value columns.
new_naming.description:
This is to keep consistency with GreptimeDB's naming pattern. By
default, this sink will use `val` for value column name, and `ts` for
time index name. When turned on, `greptime_value` and
`greptime_timestamp` will be used for these names.

If you are using this Vector sink together with other data ingestion
sources of GreptimeDB, like Prometheus Remote Write and Influxdb Line
Protocol, it is highly recommended to turn on this.

Also if there is a tag name conflict from your data source, for
example, you have a tag named as `val` or `ts`, you need to turn on
this option to avoid the conflict.

Default to `false` for compatibility.

by the [`Splunk`][splunk] API are used.

host_key.description:
By default, the [global `log_schema.host_key` option][global_host_key] is used if log
events are Legacy namespaced, or the semantic meaning of "host" is used, if defined.

[global_host_key]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#log_schema.host_key

For more information, see [Humio’s Format of Data][humio_data_format].

connection to the cluster, allowing discovery of all the other hosts in the cluster.

If the field does not exist in the log or in the tags, a blank value is used. If
unspecified, the key is not sent.

Kafka uses a hash of the key to choose the partition or uses round-robin if the record has
no key.

Configures how events are encoded into raw bytes.

endpoint.title: The base URL of the Loki instance.
endpoint.description: The `path` value is appended to this.

labels.title: A set of labels that are attached to each batch of events.
labels.description:
Both keys and values are templateable, which enables you to attach dynamic labels to events.

Valid label keys include `*`, and prefixes ending with `*`, to allow for the expansion of
objects into multiple labels. See [Label expansion][label_expansion] for more information.

Note: If the set of labels has high cardinality, this can cause drastic performance issues
with Loki. To prevent this from happening, reduce the number of unique label keys and
values.

[label_expansion]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/sinks/loki/#label-expansion

out_of_order_action.title: Out-of-order event behavior.
out_of_order_action.description:
Some sources may generate events with timestamps that aren't in chronological order. Even though the
sink sorts the events before sending them to Loki, there is a chance that another event could come in
that is out of order with the latest events sent to Loki. Prior to Loki 2.4.0, this
was not supported and would result in an error during the push request.

If you're using Loki 2.4.0 or newer, `Accept` is the preferred action, which lets Loki handle
any necessary sorting/reordering. If you're using an earlier version, then you must use `Drop`
or `RewriteTimestamp` depending on which option makes the most sense for your use case.

oneOf.title: Accept the event.
oneOf.description:
The event is not dropped and is sent without modification.

Requires Loki 2.4.0 or newer.
Rewrite the timestamp of the event to the timestamp of the latest event seen by the sink.
Drop the event.

path.description: The path to use in the URL of the Loki instance.

remove_label_fields.description: Whether or not to delete fields from the event when they are used as labels.

remove_structured_metadata_fields.description: Whether or not to delete fields from the event when they are used in structured metadata.

remove_timestamp.title: Whether or not to remove the timestamp from the event payload.
remove_timestamp.description: The timestamp is still sent as event metadata for Loki to use for indexing.

request.title: Middleware settings for outbound requests.
request.description:
Various settings can be configured, such as concurrency and rate limits, timeouts, and retry behavior.

Note that the retry backoff policy follows the Fibonacci sequence.

structured_metadata.title: Structured metadata that is attached to each batch of events.
structured_metadata.description:
Both keys and values are templateable, which enables you to attach dynamic structured metadata to events.

Valid metadata keys include `*`, and prefixes ending with `*`, to allow for the expansion of
objects into multiple metadata entries. This follows the same logic as [Label expansion][label_expansion].

[label_expansion]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/sinks/loki/#label-expansion

[tenant_id]: https://grafana.com/docs/loki/latest/operations/multi-tenancy/

batch.title: Event batching behavior.
batch.description:
Note that as PostgreSQL's `jsonb_populate_recordset` function is used to insert events,
a single event in the batch can make the whole batch to fail. For example, if a single event within the batch triggers
a unique constraint violation in the destination table, the whole event batch will fail.

As a workaround, [triggers](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/sql-createtrigger.html) on constraint violations
can be defined at a database level to change the behavior of the insert operation on specific tables.
Alternatively, setting `max_events` batch setting to `1` will make each event to be inserted independently,
so events that trigger a constraint violation will not affect the rest of the events.

endpoint.description:
The PostgreSQL server connection string. It can contain the username and password.
See [PostgreSQL documentation](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/libpq-connect.html#LIBPQ-CONNSTRING) about connection strings for more information
about valid formats and options that can be used.

pool_size.description:
The postgres connection pool size. See [this](https://docs.rs/sqlx/latest/sqlx/struct.Pool.html#why-use-a-pool) for more
information about why a connection pool should be used.

request.title: Middleware settings for outbound requests.
request.description:
Various settings can be configured, such as concurrency and rate limits, timeouts, and retry behavior.

Note that the retry backoff policy follows the Fibonacci sequence.

table.description:
The table that data is inserted into. This table parameter is vulnerable
to SQL injection attacks as Vector does not validate or sanitize it, you must not use untrusted input.
This parameter will be directly interpolated in the SQL query statement,
as table names as parameters in prepared statements are not allowed in PostgreSQL.

buckets.title: Default buckets to use for aggregating [distribution][dist_metric_docs] metrics into histograms.

default_namespace.title: The default namespace for any metrics sent.
default_namespace.description:
This namespace is only used if a metric has no existing namespace. When a namespace is
present, it is used as a prefix to the metric name, and separated with an underscore (`_`).

It should follow the Prometheus [naming conventions][prom_naming_docs].

[prom_naming_docs]: https://prometheus.io/docs/practices/naming/#metric-names

distributions_as_summaries.title: Whether or not to render [distributions][dist_metric_docs] as an [aggregated histogram][prom_agg_hist_docs] or  [aggregated summary][prom_agg_summ_docs].
distributions_as_summaries.description:
While distributions as a lossless way to represent a set of samples for a
metric is supported, Prometheus clients (the application being scraped, which is this sink) must
aggregate locally into either an aggregated histogram or aggregated summary.

[dist_metric_docs]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/data-model/metric/#distribution
[prom_agg_hist_docs]: https://prometheus.io/docs/concepts/metric_types/#histogram
[prom_agg_summ_docs]: https://prometheus.io/docs/concepts/metric_types/#summary

flush_period_secs.title: The interval, in seconds, on which metrics are flushed.
flush_period_secs.description:
On the flush interval, if a metric has not been seen since the last flush interval, it is
considered expired and is removed.

Be sure to configure this value higher than your client’s scrape interval.

quantiles.title: Quantiles to use for aggregating [distribution][dist_metric_docs] metrics into a summary.

suppress_timestamp.title: Suppresses timestamps on the Prometheus output.
suppress_timestamp.description:
This can sometimes be useful when the source of metrics leads to their timestamps being too
far in the past for Prometheus to allow them, such as when aggregating metrics over long
time periods, or when replaying old metrics from a disk buffer.

This resembles a priority queue, where messages can be pushed and popped with an
associated score.

oneOf.description: Redis channels function in a pub/sub fashion, allowing many-to-many broadcasting and receiving.

This is the default.

oneOf.title: Use the `lpush` method.
oneOf.description: This pushes messages onto the head of the list.

enabled.title: Controls whether or not end-to-end acknowledgements are enabled.
enabled.description:
When enabled for a sink, any source that supports end-to-end
acknowledgements that is connected to that sink waits for events
to be acknowledged by **all connected sinks** before acknowledging them at the source.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the sink level takes precedence over any global
[`acknowledgements`][global_acks] configuration.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements

auto_extract_timestamp.title: Passes the `auto_extract_timestamp` option to Splunk.
auto_extract_timestamp.description:
This option is only relevant to Splunk v8.x and above, and is only applied when
`endpoint_target` is set to `event`.

Setting this to `true` causes Splunk to extract the timestamp from the message text
rather than use the timestamp embedded in the event. The timestamp must be in the format
`yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss`.

vector::sinks::util::adaptive_concurrenc.title: Configuration of adaptive concurrency parameters.
vector::sinks::util::adaptive_concurrenc.description:
These parameters typically do not require changes from the default, and incorrect values can lead to meta-stable or
unstable performance and sink behavior. Proceed with caution.

decrease_ratio.title: The fraction of the current value to set the new concurrency limit when decreasing the limit.
decrease_ratio.description:
Valid values are greater than `0` and less than `1`. Smaller values cause the algorithm to scale back rapidly
when latency increases.

**Note**: The new limit is rounded down after applying this ratio.

ewma_alpha.title: The weighting of new measurements compared to older measurements.
ewma_alpha.description:
Valid values are greater than `0` and less than `1`.

ARC uses an exponentially weighted moving average (EWMA) of past RTT measurements as a reference to compare with
the current RTT. Smaller values cause this reference to adjust more slowly, which may be useful if a service has
unusually high response variability.

initial_concurrency.title: The initial concurrency limit to use. If not specified, the initial limit is 1 (no concurrency).
initial_concurrency.description:
Datadog recommends setting this value to your service's average limit if you're seeing that it takes a
long time to ramp up adaptive concurrency after a restart. You can find this value by looking at the
`adaptive_concurrency_limit` metric.

max_concurrency_limit.title: The maximum concurrency limit.
max_concurrency_limit.description: The adaptive request concurrency limit does not go above this bound. This is put in place as a safeguard.

rtt_deviation_scale.title: Scale of RTT deviations which are not considered anomalous.
rtt_deviation_scale.description:
Valid values are greater than or equal to `0`, and reasonable values range from `1.0` to `3.0`.

When calculating the past RTT average, a secondary “deviation” value is also computed that indicates how variable
those values are. That deviation is used when comparing the past RTT average to the current measurements, so we
can ignore increases in RTT that are within an expected range. This factor is used to scale up the deviation to
an appropriate range. Larger values cause the algorithm to ignore larger increases in the RTT.

max_bytes.title: The maximum size of a batch that is processed by a sink.
max_bytes.description:
This is based on the uncompressed size of the batched events, before they are
serialized or compressed.

max_events.description: The maximum size of a batch before it is flushed.

timeout_secs.description: The maximum age of a batch before it is flushed.

concurrency.description:
Configuration for outbound request concurrency.

This can be set either to one of the below enum values or to a positive integer, which denotes
a fixed concurrency limit.

rate_limit_duration_secs.description: The time window used for the `rate_limit_num` option.

rate_limit_num.description: The maximum number of requests allowed within the `rate_limit_duration_secs` time window.

retry_attempts.description: The maximum number of retries to make for failed requests.

retry_initial_backoff_secs.title: The amount of time to wait before attempting the first retry for a failed request.
retry_initial_backoff_secs.description: After the first retry has failed, the Fibonacci sequence is used to select future backoffs.

retry_jitter_mode.description: The jitter mode to use for retry backoff behavior.

retry_max_duration_secs.description: The maximum amount of time to wait between retries.

timeout_secs.title: The time a request can take before being aborted.
timeout_secs.description:
Datadog highly recommends that you do not lower this value below the service's internal timeout, as this could
create orphaned requests, pile on retries, and result in duplicate data downstream.

vector::sinks::util::retries::JitterMode.description: The jitter mode to use for retry backoff behavior.

oneOf.description: No jitter.

oneOf.title: Full jitter.
oneOf.description:
The random delay is anywhere from 0 up to the maximum current delay calculated by the backoff
strategy.

Incorporating full jitter into your backoff strategy can greatly reduce the likelihood
of creating accidental denial of service (DoS) conditions against your own systems when
many clients are recovering from a failure state.

stores blobs under a particular directory. If using a prefix for this purpose, it must end
in `/` to act as a directory path. A trailing `/` is **not** automatically added.

The final file path is in the format of `{root}/{prefix}{suffix}`.

The selected encoding also determines which input types (logs, metrics, traces) are supported.

message_buffering.description: Configuration for message buffering which enables message replay for clients that connect later.

oneOf.description: Configuration for message buffering which enables message replay for clients that connect later.

client_ack_config.description:
Configuration for ACK support for message buffering.
Enabling ACK support makes it possible to replay messages for clients without requiring query
parameters at connection time. It moves the burden of tracking latest received messages from
clients to this component. It requires clients to respond to received messages with an ACK.

Configuration for ACK support for message buffering.
Enabling ACK support makes it possible to replay messages for clients without requiring query
parameters at connection time. It moves the burden of tracking latest received messages from
clients to this component. It requires clients to respond to received messages with an ACK.

message_id_path.description:
Name of the field that contains the ACKed message ID. Use "." if message ID is the root of
the message.

max_events.title: Max events to hold in buffer.
max_events.description:
The buffer is backed by a ring buffer, so the oldest messages will be lost when the size
limit is reached.

message_id_path.title: Message ID path.
message_id_path.description:
This has to be defined to expose message ID to clients in the messages. Using that ID,
clients can request replay starting from the message ID of their choosing.

Supports only listed subprotocols. If client doesn't send any of these, server will skip
`Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` header and the client can choose to close the connection then.

supported_subprotocols.description:
List of supported `Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` values. First match out of requested
subprotocols will be accepted.

Supports only listed subprotocols. If client doesn't send any of these, server will skip
`Sec-WebSocket-Protocol` header and the client can choose to close the connection then.

acknowledgements.title: Controls how acknowledgements are handled by this source.
acknowledgements.description:
This setting is **deprecated** in favor of enabling `acknowledgements` at the [global][global_acks] or sink level.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the source level has **no effect** on acknowledgement behavior.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements
[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

decoding.description:
Configures how events are decoded from raw bytes. Note some decoders can also determine the event output
type (log, metric, trace).

framing.title: Framing configuration.
framing.description:
Framing handles how events are separated when encoded in a raw byte form, where each event is
a frame that must be prefixed, or delimited, in a way that marks where an event begins and
ends within the byte stream.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

prefetch_count.title: Maximum number of unacknowledged messages the broker will deliver to this consumer.
prefetch_count.description:
This controls flow control via AMQP QoS prefetch. Lower values limit memory usage and
prevent overwhelming slow consumers, but may reduce throughput. Higher values increase
throughput but consume more memory.

If not set, the broker/client default applies (often unlimited).

queue.description: The name of the queue to consume.

endpoints.description: The list of `mod_status` endpoints to scrape metrics from.

endpoint.title: Base URI of the task metadata endpoint.
endpoint.description:
If empty, the URI is automatically discovered based on the latest version detected.

By default:
- The version 4 endpoint base URI is stored in the environment variable `ECS_CONTAINER_METADATA_URI_V4`.
- The version 3 endpoint base URI is stored in the environment variable `ECS_CONTAINER_METADATA_URI`.
- The version 2 endpoint base URI is `169.254.170.2/v2/`.

scrape_interval_secs.description: The interval between scrapes, in seconds.

version.title: The version of the task metadata endpoint to use.
version.description:
If empty, the version is automatically discovered based on environment variables.

By default:
- Version 4 is used if the environment variable `ECS_CONTAINER_METADATA_URI_V4` is defined.
- Version 3 is used if the environment variable `ECS_CONTAINER_METADATA_URI_V4` is not defined, but the
  environment variable `ECS_CONTAINER_METADATA_URI` _is_ defined.
- Version 2 is used if neither of the environment variables `ECS_CONTAINER_METADATA_URI_V4` or
  `ECS_CONTAINER_METADATA_URI` are defined.

oneOf.title: Version 2.
oneOf.description:
More information about version 2 of the task metadata endpoint can be found [here][endpoint_v2].

[endpoint_v2]: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/task-metadata-endpoint-v2.html

oneOf.title: Version 3.
oneOf.description:
More information about version 3 of the task metadata endpoint can be found [here][endpoint_v3].

[endpoint_v3]: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/task-metadata-endpoint-v3.html

oneOf.title: Version 4.
oneOf.description:
More information about version 4 of the task metadata endpoint can be found [here][endpoint_v4].

[endpoint_v4]: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/developerguide/task-metadata-endpoint-v4.html

configured, `access_key` should be set to the same value. Otherwise, all requests are allowed.

keepalive.description: Configuration of HTTP server keepalive parameters.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

record_compression.title: The compression scheme to use for decompressing records within the Firehose message.
record_compression.description:
Some services, like AWS CloudWatch Logs, [compresses the events with gzip][events_with_gzip],
before sending them AWS Kinesis Firehose. This option can be used to automatically decompress
them before forwarding them to the next component.

Note that this is different from [Content encoding option][encoding_option] of the
Firehose HTTP endpoint destination. That option controls the content encoding of the entire HTTP request.

[events_with_gzip]: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/firehose/latest/dev/writing-with-cloudwatch-logs.html
[encoding_option]: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/firehose/latest/dev/create-destination.html#create-destination-http

oneOf.title: Automatically attempt to determine the compression scheme.
oneOf.description:
The compression scheme of the object is determined by looking at its file signature, also known
as [magic bytes][magic_bytes].

If the record fails to decompress with the discovered format, the record is forwarded as is.
Thus, if you know the records are always gzip encoded (for example, if they are coming from AWS CloudWatch Logs),
set `gzip` in this field so that any records that are not-gzipped are rejected.

[magic_bytes]: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_file_signatures
Uncompressed.
GZIP.

store_access_key.title: Whether or not to store the AWS Firehose Access Key in event secrets.
store_access_key.description:
If set to `true`, when incoming requests contains an access key sent by AWS Firehose, it is kept in the
event secrets as "aws_kinesis_firehose_access_key".

It is set to `none` if the compression scheme cannot be determined.

multiline.description: If not specified, multiline aggregation is disabled.

oneOf.description: SQS configuration options.

client_concurrency.title: Number of concurrent tasks to create for polling the queue for messages.
client_concurrency.description:
Defaults to the number of available CPUs on the system.

Should not typically need to be changed, but it can sometimes be beneficial to raise this
value when there is a high rate of messages being pushed into the queue and the objects
being fetched are small. In these cases, system resources may not be fully utilized without
fetching more messages per second, as the SQS message consumption rate affects the S3 object
retrieval rate.

deferred.description: Configuration for deferring events to another queue based on their age.

oneOf.description: Configuration for deferring events based on their age.

max_age_secs.title: Event must have been emitted within the last `max_age_secs` seconds to be processed.
max_age_secs.description: If the event is older, it is forwarded to the `queue_url` for later processing.

queue_url.description: The URL of the queue to forward events to when they are older than `max_age_secs`.

delete_failed_message.title: Whether to delete non-retryable messages.
delete_failed_message.description: If a message is rejected by the sink and not retryable, it is deleted from the queue.

delete_message.title: Whether to delete the message once it is processed.
delete_message.description: It can be useful to set this to `false` for debugging or during the initial setup.

max_number_of_messages.title: Maximum number of messages to poll from SQS in a batch
max_number_of_messages.description:
Defaults to 10

Should be set to a smaller value when the files are large to help prevent the ingestion of
one file from causing the other files to exceed the visibility_timeout. Valid values are 1 - 10

poll_secs.title: How long to wait while polling the queue for new messages, in seconds.
poll_secs.description:
Generally, this should not be changed unless instructed to do so, as if messages are available,
they are always consumed, regardless of the value of `poll_secs`.

queue_url.description: The URL of the SQS queue to poll for bucket notifications.

visibility_timeout_secs.title: The visibility timeout to use for messages, in seconds.
visibility_timeout_secs.description:
This controls how long a message is left unavailable after it is received. If a message is received, and
takes longer than `visibility_timeout_secs` to process and delete the message from the queue, it is made available again for another consumer.

This can happen if there is an issue between consuming a message and deleting it.

allOf.description: Client timeout configuration for AWS operations.

oneOf.description: Client timeout configuration for AWS operations.

connect_timeout_seconds.title: The connection timeout for AWS requests
connect_timeout_seconds.description: Limits the amount of time allowed to initiate a socket connection.

operation_timeout_seconds.title: The operation timeout for AWS requests
operation_timeout_seconds.description:
Limits the amount of time allowed for an operation to be fully serviced; an
operation represents the full request/response lifecycle of a call to a service.
Take care when configuring this settings to allow enough time for the polling
interval configured in `poll_secs`

read_timeout_seconds.title: The read timeout for AWS requests
read_timeout_seconds.description:
Limits the amount of time allowed to read the first byte of a response from the
time the request is initiated. Take care when configuring this settings to allow
enough time for the polling interval configured in `poll_secs`

strategy.description: The strategy to use to consume objects from S3.

address.description: It _must_ include a port.

decoding.description:
Configures how events are decoded from raw bytes. Note some decoders can also determine the event output
type (log, metric, trace).

disable_logs.description: If this is set to `true`, logs are not accepted by the component.

disable_metrics.description: If this is set to `true`, metrics (beta) are not accepted by the component.

disable_traces.description: If this is set to `true`, traces (alpha) are not accepted by the component.

framing.title: Framing configuration.
framing.description:
Framing handles how events are separated when encoded in a raw byte form, where each event is
a frame that must be prefixed, or delimited, in a way that marks where an event begins and
ends within the byte stream.

keepalive.description: Configuration of HTTP server keepalive parameters.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

multiple_outputs.title: If this is set to `true`, logs, metrics (beta), and traces (alpha) are sent to different outputs.
multiple_outputs.description:
For a source component named `agent`, the received logs, metrics (beta), and traces (alpha) can then be
configured as input to other components by specifying `agent.logs`, `agent.metrics`, and
`agent.traces`, respectively.

parse_ddtags.description:
If this is set to `true`, when log events contain the field `ddtags`, the string value that
contains a list of key:value pairs set by the Agent is parsed and expanded into an array.

send_timeout_secs.title: The timeout before responding to requests with a HTTP 503 Service Unavailable error.
send_timeout_secs.description:
If not set, responses to completed requests will block indefinitely until connected
transforms or sinks are ready to receive the events. When this happens, the sending Datadog
Agent will eventually time out the request and drop the connection, resulting Vector
generating an "Events dropped." error and incrementing the `component_discarded_events_total`
internal metric. By setting this option to a value less than the Agent's timeout, Vector
will instead respond to the Agent with a HTTP 503 Service Unavailable error, emit a warning,
and increment the `component_timed_out_events_total` internal metric instead.

split_metric_namespace.description:
If this is set to `true`, metric names are split at the first '.' into a namespace and name.
For example, `system.cpu.usage` would be split into namespace `system` and name `cpu.usage`.
If `false`, the full metric name is used without splitting. This may be useful if you are using a
default namespace for metrics in sinks connected to this source.

store_api_key.description:
If this is set to `true`, when incoming events contain a Datadog API key, it is
stored in the event metadata and used if the event is sent to a Datadog sink.

count.title: The total number of lines to output.
count.description: By default, the source continuously prints logs (infinitely).

decoding.description:
Configures how events are decoded from raw bytes. Note some decoders can also determine the event output
type (log, metric, trace).

framing.title: Framing configuration.
framing.description:
Framing handles how events are separated when encoded in a raw byte form, where each event is
a frame that must be prefixed, or delimited, in a way that marks where an event begins and
ends within the byte stream.

interval.title: The amount of time, in seconds, to pause between each batch of output lines.
interval.description:
The default is one batch per second. To remove the delay and output batches as quickly as possible, set
`interval` to `0.0`.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

oneOf.description: Lines are chosen at random from the list specified using `lines`.

format.description: Lines are chosen at random from the list specified using `lines`.

lines.description: The list of lines to output.

sequence.description: If `true`, each output line starts with an increasing sequence number, beginning with 0.

By default, the [global `log_schema.host_key` option][global_host_key] is used.

max_frame_length.title: Maximum DNSTAP frame length that the source accepts.
max_frame_length.description: If any frame is longer than this, it is discarded.

raw_data_only.title: Whether or not to skip parsing or decoding of DNSTAP frames.
raw_data_only.description:
If set to `true`, frames are not parsed or decoded. The raw frame data is set as a field on the event
(called `rawData`) and encoded as a base64 string.

The socket address to listen for connections on, or `systemd{#N}` to use the Nth socket passed by
systemd socket activation.

connection_limit.description: The maximum number of TCP connections that are allowed at any given time.

max_connection_duration_secs.title: Maximum duration to keep each connection open. Connections open for longer than this duration are closed.
max_connection_duration_secs.description: This is helpful for load balancing long-lived connections.

port_key.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the peer host's port to each event.
port_key.description:
The value will be the peer host's port i.e. `9000`.

By default, `"port"` is used.

Set to `""` to suppress this key.

receive_buffer_bytes.description: The size of the receive buffer used for each connection.

shutdown_timeout_secs.description: The timeout before a connection is forcefully closed during shutdown.

Note: The file mode value can be specified in any numeric format supported by your configuration
language, but it is most intuitive to use an octal number.

socket_path.title: Absolute path to the socket file to read DNSTAP data from.
socket_path.description:
The DNS server must be configured to send its DNSTAP data to this socket file. The socket file is created
if it doesn't already exist when the source first starts.

socket_receive_buffer_size.title: The size, in bytes, of the receive buffer used for the socket.
socket_receive_buffer_size.description: This should not typically needed to be changed.

socket_send_buffer_size.title: The size, in bytes, of the send buffer used for the socket.
socket_send_buffer_size.description: This should not typically needed to be changed.

auto_partial_merge.description: Enables automatic merging of partial events.

docker_host.title: Docker host to connect to.
docker_host.description:
Use an HTTPS URL to enable TLS encryption.

If absent, the `DOCKER_HOST` environment variable is used. If `DOCKER_HOST` is also absent,
the default Docker local socket (`/var/run/docker.sock` on Unix platforms,
`//./pipe/docker_engine` on Windows) is used.

exclude_containers.title: A list of container IDs or names of containers to exclude from log collection.
exclude_containers.description:
Matching is prefix first, so specifying a value of `foo` would match any container named `foo` as well as any
container whose name started with `foo`. This applies equally whether matching container IDs or names.

By default, the source collects logs for all containers. If `exclude_containers` is configured, any
container that matches a configured exclusion is excluded even if it is also included with
`include_containers`, so care should be taken when using prefix matches as they cannot be overridden by a
corresponding entry in `include_containers`, for example, excluding `foo` by attempting to include `foo-specific-id`.

This can be used in conjunction with `include_containers`.

host_key.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the current hostname to each event.
host_key.description:
By default, the [global `log_schema.host_key` option][global_host_key] is used.

[global_host_key]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#log_schema.host_key

include_containers.title: A list of container IDs or names of containers to include in log collection.
include_containers.description:
Matching is prefix first, so specifying a value of `foo` would match any container named `foo` as well as any
container whose name started with `foo`. This applies equally whether matching container IDs or names.

By default, the source collects logs for all containers. If `include_containers` is configured, only
containers that match a configured inclusion and are also not excluded get matched.

This can be used in conjunction with `exclude_containers`.

include_images.title: A list of image names to match against.
include_images.description: If not provided, all images are included.

include_labels.title: A list of container object labels to match against when filtering running containers.
include_labels.description: Labels should follow the syntax described in the [Docker object labels](https://docs.docker.com/config/labels-custom-metadata/) documentation.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

multiline.title: Multiline aggregation configuration.
multiline.description: If not specified, multiline aggregation is disabled.

partial_event_marker_field.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to mark an event as partial.
partial_event_marker_field.description:
If `auto_partial_merge` is disabled, partial events are emitted with a log field, set by this
configuration value, indicating that the event is not complete.

retry_backoff_secs.description: The amount of time to wait before retrying after an error.

tls.title: Configuration of TLS when connecting to the Docker daemon.
tls.description:
Only relevant when connecting to Docker with an HTTPS URL.

If not configured, the environment variable `DOCKER_CERT_PATH` is used. If `DOCKER_CERT_PATH` is absent, then` DOCKER_CONFIG` is used. If both environment variables are absent, the certificates in `~/.docker/` are read.

oneOf.title: Configuration of TLS when connecting to the Docker daemon.
oneOf.description:
Only relevant when connecting to Docker with an HTTPS URL.

If not configured, the environment variable `DOCKER_CERT_PATH` is used. If `DOCKER_CERT_PATH` is absent, then` DOCKER_CONFIG` is used. If both environment variables are absent, the certificates in `~/.docker/` are read.

crt_file.description: Path to the TLS certificate file.

acknowledgements.title: Controls how acknowledgements are handled by this source.
acknowledgements.description:
This setting is **deprecated** in favor of enabling `acknowledgements` at the [global][global_acks] or sink level.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the source level has **no effect** on acknowledgement behavior.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements
[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

data_dir.title: The directory used to persist file checkpoint positions.
data_dir.description:
By default, the [global `data_dir` option][global_data_dir] is used.
Make sure the running user has write permissions to this directory.

If this directory is specified, then Vector will attempt to create it.

[global_data_dir]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#data_dir

encoding.description: Character set encoding.

oneOf.description: Character set encoding.

charset.title: Encoding of the source messages.
charset.description:
Takes one of the encoding [label strings](https://encoding.spec.whatwg.org/#concept-encoding-get) defined as
part of the [Encoding Standard](https://encoding.spec.whatwg.org/).

When set, the messages are transcoded from the specified encoding to UTF-8, which is the encoding that is
assumed internally for string-like data. Enable this transcoding operation if you need your data to
be in UTF-8 for further processing. At the time of transcoding, any malformed sequences (that can't be mapped to
UTF-8) is replaced with the Unicode [REPLACEMENT
CHARACTER](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Specials_(Unicode_block)#Replacement_character) and warnings are
logged.

exclude.title: Array of file patterns to exclude. [Globbing](https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/sources/file/#globbing) is supported.
exclude.description:
Takes precedence over the `include` option. Note: The `exclude` patterns are applied _after_ the attempt to glob everything
in `include`. This means that all files are first matched by `include` and then filtered by the `exclude`
patterns. This can be impactful if `include` contains directories with contents that are not accessible.

The value is the full path to the file where the event was read message.

Set to `""` to suppress this key.

fingerprint.title: Configuration for how files should be identified.
fingerprint.description: This is important for `checkpointing` when file rotation is used.

oneOf.description: Read lines from the beginning of the file and compute a checksum over them.

ignored_header_bytes.title:
The number of bytes to skip ahead (or ignore) when reading the data used for generating the checksum.
If the file is compressed, the number of bytes refer to the header in the uncompressed content. Only
gzip is supported at this time.
ignored_header_bytes.description: This can be helpful if all files share a common header that should be skipped.

lines.title: The number of lines to read for generating the checksum.
lines.description:
The number of lines are determined from the uncompressed content if the file is compressed. Only
gzip is supported at this time.

If the file has less than this amount of lines, it won’t be read at all.

vector::sources::host_metrics::PatternWr.title: A compiled Unix shell-style pattern.
vector::sources::host_metrics::PatternWr.description:
- `?` matches any single character.
- `*` matches any (possibly empty) sequence of characters.
- `**` matches the current directory and arbitrary subdirectories. This sequence must form a single path component,
  so both `**a` and `b**` are invalid and will result in an error. A sequence of more than two consecutive `*`
  characters is also invalid.
- `[...]` matches any character inside the brackets. Character sequences can also specify ranges of characters, as
  ordered by Unicode, so e.g. `[0-9]` specifies any character between 0 and 9 inclusive. An unclosed bracket is
  invalid.
- `[!...]` is the negation of `[...]`, i.e. it matches any characters not in the brackets.

The metacharacters `?`, `*`, `[`, `]` can be matched by using brackets (e.g. `[?]`). When a `]` occurs immediately
following `[` or `[!` then it is interpreted as being part of, rather then ending, the character set, so `]` and NOT
`]` can be matched by `[]]` and `[!]]` respectively. The `-` character can be specified inside a character sequence
pattern by placing it at the start or the end, e.g. `[abc-]`.

When a body is provided, the `Content-Type` header is automatically set to

The parameters provided in this option are appended to any parameters
manually provided in the `endpoint` option.

VRL functions are supported within query parameters. You can
use functions like `now()` to dynamically modify query

The interval between scrapes. Requests are run concurrently so if a scrape takes longer
than the interval a new scrape will be started. This can take extra resources, set the timeout
to a value lower than the scrape interval to prevent this from happening.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

address.title: The socket address to listen for connections on.
address.description: It _must_ include a port.

encoding.title: The expected encoding of received data.
encoding.description: For `json` and `ndjson` encodings, the fields of the JSON objects are output as separate fields.

headers.title: A list of HTTP headers to include in the log event.
headers.description:
Accepts the wildcard (`*`) character for headers matching a specified pattern.

Specifying "*" results in all headers included in the log event.

These headers are not included in the JSON payload if a field with a conflicting name exists.

host_key.description: If set, the name of the log field used to add the remote IP to each event

keepalive.description: Configuration of HTTP server keepalive parameters.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

method.description: Specifies the action of the HTTP request.

path.description: The URL path on which log event POST requests are sent.

path_key.description: The event key in which the requested URL path used to send the request is stored.

query_parameters.title: A list of URL query parameters to include in the log event.
query_parameters.description:
Accepts the wildcard (`*`) character for query parameters matching a specified pattern.

Specifying "*" results in all query parameters included in the log event.

These override any values included in the body with conflicting names.

response_code.description: Specifies the HTTP response status code that will be returned on successful requests.

strict_path.title: Whether or not to treat the configured `path` as an absolute path.
strict_path.description:
If set to `true`, only requests using the exact URL path specified in `path` are accepted. Otherwise,
requests sent to a URL path that starts with the value of `path` are accepted.

With `strict_path` set to `false` and `path` set to `""`, the configured HTTP source accepts requests from
any URL path.

host_key.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the current hostname to each event.
host_key.description:
By default, the [global `log_schema.host_key` option][global_host_key] is used.

Set to `""` to suppress this key.

[global_host_key]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#log_schema.host_key

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

pid_key.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the current process ID to each event.
pid_key.description:
By default, `"pid"` is used.

Set to `""` to suppress this key.

namespace.description: Overrides the default namespace for the metrics emitted by the source.

scrape_interval_secs.description: The interval between metric gathering, in seconds.

host_key.title: Overrides the name of the tag used to add the peer host to each metric.
host_key.description:
The value is the peer host's address, including the port. For example, `1.2.3.4:9000`.

By default, the [global `log_schema.host_key` option][global_host_key] is used.

Set to `""` to suppress this key.

[global_host_key]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#log_schema.host_key

pid_key.title: Sets the name of the tag to use to add the current process ID to each metric.
pid_key.description: By default, this is not set and the tag is not automatically added.

acknowledgements.title: Controls how acknowledgements are handled by this source.
acknowledgements.description:
This setting is **deprecated** in favor of enabling `acknowledgements` at the [global][global_acks] or sink level.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the source level has **no effect** on acknowledgement behavior.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements
[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

batch_size.title: The systemd journal is read in batches, and a checkpoint is set at the end of each batch.
batch_size.description: This option limits the size of the batch.

current_boot_only.description: Only include entries that occurred after the current boot of the system.

data_dir.title: The directory used to persist file checkpoint positions.
data_dir.description:
By default, the [global `data_dir` option][global_data_dir] is used.
Make sure the running user has write permissions to this directory.

If this directory is specified, then Vector will attempt to create it.

[global_data_dir]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#data_dir

emit_cursor.title: Whether to emit the [__CURSOR field][cursor]. See also [sd_journal_get_cursor][get_cursor].

exclude_matches.title:
A list of sets of field/value pairs that, if any are present in a journal entry,
excludes the entry from this source.
exclude_matches.description: If `exclude_units` is specified, it is merged into this list.

_metadata.docs::additional_props_description: The set of field values to match in journal entries that are to be excluded.

exclude_units.title: A list of unit names to exclude from monitoring.
exclude_units.description:
Unit names lacking a `.` have `.service` appended to make them a valid service unit
name.

extra_args.title: A list of extra command line arguments to pass to `journalctl`.
extra_args.description: If specified, it is merged to the command line arguments as-is.

include_matches.title: A list of sets of field/value pairs to monitor.
include_matches.description:
If empty or not present, all journal fields are accepted.

If `include_units` is specified, it is merged into this list.

_metadata.docs::additional_props_description: The set of field values to match in journal entries that are to be included.

include_units.title: A list of unit names to monitor.
include_units.description:
If empty or not present, all units are accepted.

Unit names lacking a `.` have `.service` appended to make them a valid service unit name.

**Note:** This option matches only the `_SYSTEMD_UNIT` field, which is narrower than `journalctl --unit`.
Messages from systemd about unit lifecycle (start/stop) have `_SYSTEMD_UNIT=init.scope` and will not match.
To capture these, explicitly include `init.scope` or use `include_matches` for finer control.

journal_directory.title: The full path of the journal directory.
journal_directory.description: If not set, `journalctl` uses the default system journal path.

journal_namespace.title: The [journal namespace][journal-namespace].
journal_namespace.description:
This value is passed to `journalctl` through the [`--namespace` option][journalctl-namespace-option].
If not set, `journalctl` uses the default namespace.

[journal-namespace]: https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd-journald.service.html#Journal%20Namespaces
[journalctl-namespace-option]: https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/journalctl.html#--namespace=NAMESPACE

journalctl_path.title: The full path of the `journalctl` executable.
journalctl_path.description: If not set, a search is done for the `journalctl` path.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

remap_priority.title: Enables remapping the `PRIORITY` field from an integer to string value.
remap_priority.description: Has no effect unless the value of the field is already an integer.

_metadata.deprecated_message: This option has been deprecated, use the `remap` transform and `to_syslog_level` function instead.

since_now.description: Only include entries that appended to the journal after the entries have been read.

acknowledgements.title: Controls how acknowledgements are handled by this source.
acknowledgements.description:
This setting is **deprecated** in favor of enabling `acknowledgements` at the [global][global_acks] or sink level.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the source level has **no effect** on acknowledgement behavior.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements
[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

auto_offset_reset.title: If offsets for consumer group do not exist, set them using this strategy.
auto_offset_reset.description: See the [librdkafka documentation](https://github.com/edenhill/librdkafka/blob/master/CONFIGURATION.md) for the `auto.offset.reset` option for further clarification.

bootstrap_servers.title: A comma-separated list of Kafka bootstrap servers.
bootstrap_servers.description:
These are the servers in a Kafka cluster that a client should use to bootstrap its connection to the cluster,
allowing discovery of all the other hosts in the cluster.

Must be in the form of `host:port`, and comma-separated.

commit_interval_ms.description: The frequency that the consumer offsets are committed (written) to offset storage.

decoding.description:
Configures how events are decoded from raw bytes. Note some decoders can also determine the event output
type (log, metric, trace).

drain_timeout_ms.title:
Timeout to drain pending acknowledgements during shutdown or a Kafka
consumer group rebalance.
drain_timeout_ms.description:
When Vector shuts down or the Kafka consumer group revokes partitions from this
consumer, wait a maximum of `drain_timeout_ms` for the source to
process pending acknowledgements. Must be less than `session_timeout_ms`
to ensure the consumer is not excluded from the group during a rebalance.

Default value is half of `session_timeout_ms`.

fetch_wait_max_ms.description: Maximum time the broker may wait to fill the response.

framing.title: Framing configuration.
framing.description:
Framing handles how events are separated when encoded in a raw byte form, where each event is
a frame that must be prefixed, or delimited, in a way that marks where an event begins and
ends within the byte stream.

group_id.description: The consumer group name to be used to consume events from Kafka.

headers_key.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the headers to each event.
headers_key.description:
The value is the headers of the Kafka message itself.

By default, `"headers"` is used.

key_field.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the message key to each event.
key_field.description:
The value is the message key of the Kafka message itself.

By default, `"message_key"` is used.

librdkafka_options.title: Advanced options set directly on the underlying `librdkafka` client.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

topic_lag_metric.description: Expose topic lag metrics for all topics and partitions. Metric names are `kafka_consumer_lag`.

offset_key.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the offset to each event.
offset_key.description:
The value is the offset of the Kafka message itself.

By default, `"offset"` is used.

partition_key.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the partition to each event.
partition_key.description:
The value is the partition from which the Kafka message was consumed from.

By default, `"partition"` is used.

topic_key.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the topic to each event.
topic_key.description:
The value is the topic from which the Kafka message was consumed from.

By default, `"topic"` is used.

topics.title: The Kafka topics names to read events from.
topics.description: Regular expression syntax is supported if the topic begins with `^`.

auto_partial_merge.title: Whether or not to automatically merge partial events.
auto_partial_merge.description:
Partial events are messages that were split by the Kubernetes Container Runtime
log driver.

data_dir.title: The directory used to persist file checkpoint positions.
data_dir.description:
By default, the [global `data_dir` option][global_data_dir] is used.
Make sure the running user has write permissions to this directory.

If this directory is specified, then Vector will attempt to create it.

[global_data_dir]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#data_dir

delay_deletion_ms.title:
How long to delay removing metadata entries from the cache when a pod deletion event
event is received from the watch stream.
delay_deletion_ms.description:
A longer delay allows for continued enrichment of logs after the originating Pod is
removed. If relevant metadata has been removed, the log is forwarded un-enriched and a
warning is emitted.

exclude_paths_glob_patterns.description: A list of glob patterns to exclude from reading the files.

extra_field_selector.title:
Specifies the [field selector][field_selector] to filter Pods with, to be used in addition
to the built-in [Node][node] filter.
extra_field_selector.description:
The built-in Node filter uses `self_node_name` to only watch Pods located on the same Node.

[field_selector]: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/overview/working-with-objects/field-selectors/
[node]: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/architecture/nodes/

extra_label_selector.title:
Specifies the [label selector][label_selector] to filter [Pods][pods] with, to be used in
addition to the built-in [exclude][exclude] filter.

extra_namespace_label_selector.title:
Specifies the [label selector][label_selector] to filter [Namespaces][namespaces] with, to
be used in addition to the built-in [exclude][exclude] filter.

fingerprint_lines.title: The number of lines to read for generating the checksum.
fingerprint_lines.description:
If your files share a common header that is not always a fixed size,

If the file has less than this amount of lines, it won't be read at all.

glob_minimum_cooldown_ms.title: The interval at which the file system is polled to identify new files to read from.
glob_minimum_cooldown_ms.description:
This is quite efficient, yet might still create some load on the
file system; in addition, it is currently coupled with checksum dumping
in the underlying file server, so setting it too low may introduce
a significant overhead.

ignore_older_secs.description: Ignore files with a data modification date older than the specified number of seconds.

include_paths_glob_patterns.description: A list of glob patterns to include while reading the files.

ingestion_timestamp_field.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the ingestion timestamp to each event.
ingestion_timestamp_field.description:
This is useful to compute the latency between important event processing
stages. For example, the time delta between when a log line was written and when it was
processed by the `kubernetes_logs` source.

insert_namespace_fields.title: Specifies whether or not to enrich logs with namespace fields.
insert_namespace_fields.description:
Setting to `false` prevents Vector from pulling in namespaces and thus namespace label fields will not
be available. This helps reduce load on the `kube-apiserver` and lowers daemonset memory usage in clusters
with many namespaces.

internal_metrics.description: Configuration of internal metrics for file-based components.

kube_config_file.title: Optional path to a readable [kubeconfig][kubeconfig] file.
kube_config_file.description:
If not set, a connection to Kubernetes is made using the in-cluster configuration.

[kubeconfig]: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/organize-cluster-access-kubeconfig/

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

max_line_bytes.title: The maximum number of bytes a line can contain before being discarded.
max_line_bytes.description: This protects against malformed lines or tailing incorrect files.

max_merged_line_bytes.title: The maximum number of bytes a line can contain - after merging - before being discarded.
max_merged_line_bytes.description:
This protects against malformed lines or tailing incorrect files.

Note that, if auto_partial_merge is false, this config will be ignored. Also, if max_line_bytes is too small to reach the continuation character, then this
config will have no practical impact (the same is true of `auto_partial_merge`). Finally, the smaller of `max_merged_line_bytes` and `max_line_bytes` will apply
if auto_partial_merge is true, so if this is set to be 1 MiB, for example, but `max_line_bytes` is set to ~2.5 MiB, then every line greater than 1 MiB will be dropped.

max_read_bytes.title:
Max amount of bytes to read from a single file before switching over to the next file.
**Note:** This does not apply when `oldest_first` is `true`.
max_read_bytes.description:
This allows distributing the reads more or less evenly across
the files.

namespace_annotation_fields.description: Configuration for how the events are enriched with Namespace metadata.

namespace_labels.title: Event field for the Namespace's labels.
namespace_labels.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

node_annotation_fields.description: Configuration for how the events are enriched with Node metadata.

node_labels.title: Event field for the Node's labels.
node_labels.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

oldest_first.description: Instead of balancing read capacity fairly across all watched files, prioritize draining the oldest files before moving on to read data from more recent files.

pod_annotation_fields.description: Configuration for how the events are enriched with Pod metadata.

container_id.title: Event field for the Container's ID.
container_id.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

container_image.title: Event field for the Container's image.
container_image.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

container_image_id.title: Event field for the Container's image ID.
container_image_id.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

container_name.title: Event field for the Container's name.
container_name.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

pod_annotations.title: Event field for the Pod's annotations.
pod_annotations.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

pod_ip.title: Event field for the Pod's IPv4 address.
pod_ip.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

pod_ips.title: Event field for the Pod's IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.
pod_ips.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

pod_labels.title: Event field for the `Pod`'s labels.
pod_labels.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

pod_name.title: Event field for the Pod's name.
pod_name.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

pod_namespace.title: Event field for the Pod's namespace.
pod_namespace.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

pod_node_name.title: Event field for the Pod's node_name.
pod_node_name.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

pod_owner.title: Event field for the Pod's owner reference.
pod_owner.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

pod_uid.title: Event field for the Pod's UID.
pod_uid.description: Set to `""` to suppress this key.

read_from.description: File position to use when reading a new file.

rotate_wait_secs.description:
How long to keep an open handle to a rotated log file.
The default value represents "no limit"

self_node_name.title: The name of the Kubernetes [Node][node] that is running.
self_node_name.description:
Configured to use an environment variable by default, to be evaluated to a value provided by
Kubernetes at Pod creation.

[node]: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/architecture/nodes/

timezone.description: The default time zone for timestamps without an explicit zone.

use_apiserver_cache.description: Determines if requests to the kube-apiserver can be served by a cache.

acknowledgements.title: Controls how acknowledgements are handled by this source.
acknowledgements.description:
This setting is **deprecated** in favor of enabling `acknowledgements` at the [global][global_acks] or sink level.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the source level has **no effect** on acknowledgement behavior.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements
[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

address.title:
The socket address to listen for connections on, or `systemd{#N}` to use the Nth socket passed by
systemd socket activation.
address.description: If a socket address is used, it _must_ include a port.

connection_limit.description: The maximum number of TCP connections that are allowed at any given time.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

receive_buffer_bytes.description: The size of the receive buffer used for each connection.

endpoints.title: A list of MongoDB instances to scrape.
endpoints.description: Each endpoint must be in the [Connection String URI Format](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/reference/connection-string/).

namespace.title: Overrides the default namespace for the metrics emitted by the source.
namespace.description:
If set to an empty string, no namespace is added to the metrics.

By default, `mongodb` is used.

scrape_interval_secs.description: The interval between scrapes, in seconds.

decoding.description:
Configures how events are decoded from raw bytes. Note some decoders can also determine the event output
type (log, metric, trace).

framing.title: Framing configuration.
framing.description:
Framing handles how events are separated when encoded in a raw byte form, where each event is
a frame that must be prefixed, or delimited, in a way that marks where an event begins and
ends within the byte stream.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

topic.description: MQTT topic or topics from which messages are to be read.

topic_key.title: Overrides the name of the log field used to add the topic to each event.
topic_key.description:
The value is the topic from which the MQTT message was published to.

By default, `"topic"` is used.

allOf.description: Shared MQTT configuration for sources and sinks.

connection_name.title: A [name][nats_connection_name] assigned to the NATS connection.

decoding.description:
Configures how events are decoded from raw bytes. Note some decoders can also determine the event output
type (log, metric, trace).

framing.title: Framing configuration.
framing.description:
Framing handles how events are separated when encoded in a raw byte form, where each event is
a frame that must be prefixed, or delimited, in a way that marks where an event begins and
ends within the byte stream.

oneOf.description: Configuration for NATS JetStream.

batch_config.title: Batch settings for a JetStream pull consumer.
batch_config.description:
By default, messages are pulled in batches of up to 200.
Each pull request expires after 30 seconds if not fulfilled.
There is no explicit maximum byte size per batch unless specified.

**Note:** These defaults follow the `async-nats` crate’s `StreamBuilder`.

batch.description: The maximum number of messages to pull in a single batch.

max_bytes.description:
The maximum total byte size for a batch. The pull request will be
fulfilled when either `size` or `max_bytes` is reached.

consumer.description: The name of the durable consumer to pull from.

stream.description: The name of the stream to bind to.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

queue.description: The NATS queue group to join.

subject.title: The NATS [subject][nats_subject] to pull messages from.

subscriber_capacity.title: The buffer capacity of the underlying NATS subscriber.
subscriber_capacity.description:
This value determines how many messages the NATS subscriber buffers
before incoming messages are dropped.

See the [async_nats documentation][async_nats_subscription_capacity] for more information.

[async_nats_subscription_capacity]: https://docs.rs/async-nats/latest/async_nats/struct.ConnectOptions.html#method.subscription_capacity

url.title: The NATS URL to connect to.
url.description:
The URL takes the form of `nats://server:port`.
If the port is not specified it defaults to 4222.

endpoints.title: A list of NGINX instances to scrape.
endpoints.description:
Each endpoint must be a valid HTTP/HTTPS URI pointing to an NGINX instance that has the
`ngx_http_stub_status_module` module enabled.

namespace.title: Overrides the default namespace for the metrics emitted by the source.
namespace.description:
If set to an empty string, no namespace is added to the metrics.

By default, `nginx` is used.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

scrape_interval_secs.description:
The interval between scrapes. Requests are run concurrently so if a scrape takes longer
than the interval, a new scrape will be started. This can take extra resources, set the timeout
to a value lower than the scrape interval to prevent this from happening.

scrape_timeout_secs.description: The timeout for each scrape request.

since.description:
The time to look back for logs. This is used to determine the start time of the first request
(that is, the earliest log to fetch)

acknowledgements.title: Controls how acknowledgements are handled by this source.
acknowledgements.description:
This setting is **deprecated** in favor of enabling `acknowledgements` at the [global][global_acks] or sink level.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the source level has **no effect** on acknowledgement behavior.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements
[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

address.title: The socket address to listen for connections on.
address.description: It _must_ include a port.

address.title: The socket address to listen for connections on.
address.description: It _must_ include a port.

headers.title: A list of HTTP headers to include in the log event.
headers.description:
Accepts the wildcard (`*`) character for headers matching a specified pattern.

Specifying "*" results in all headers included in the log event.

These headers are not included in the JSON payload if a field with a conflicting name exists.

keepalive.description: Configuration of HTTP server keepalive parameters.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

use_otlp_decoding.title: Setting this field will override the legacy mapping of OTEL protos to Vector events and use the proto directly.
use_otlp_decoding.description:
One major caveat here is that the incoming metrics will be parsed as logs but they will preserve the OTLP format.
This means that components that work on metrics, will not be compatible with this output.
However, these events can be forwarded directly to a downstream OTEL collector.

endpoints.title: A list of PostgreSQL instances to scrape.
endpoints.description:
Each endpoint must be in the [Connection URI
format](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/libpq-connect.html#id-1.7.3.8.3.6).

exclude_databases.title:
A list of databases to match (by using [POSIX Regular
Expressions](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/functions-matching.html#FUNCTIONS-POSIX-REGEXP)) against
the `datname` column for which you don’t want to collect metrics from.
exclude_databases.description:
Specifying `""` includes metrics where `datname` is `NULL`.

This can be used in conjunction with `include_databases`.

include_databases.title:
A list of databases to match (by using [POSIX Regular
Expressions](https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/functions-matching.html#FUNCTIONS-POSIX-REGEXP)) against
the `datname` column for which you want to collect metrics from.
include_databases.description:
If not set, metrics are collected from all databases. Specifying `""` includes metrics where `datname` is
`NULL`.

This can be used in conjunction with `exclude_databases`.

namespace.description: Overrides the default namespace for the metrics emitted by the source.

tls.description: Configuration of TLS when connecting to PostgreSQL.

oneOf.description: Configuration of TLS when connecting to PostgreSQL.

ca_file.title: Absolute path to an additional CA certificate file.
ca_file.description: The certificate must be in the DER or PEM (X.509) format.

acknowledgements.title: Controls how acknowledgements are handled by this source.
acknowledgements.description:
This setting is **deprecated** in favor of enabling `acknowledgements` at the [global][global_acks] or sink level.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the source level has **no effect** on acknowledgement behavior.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements
[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

address.title: The socket address to accept connections on.
address.description: The address _must_ include a port.

aggregate_metrics.title: Whether to aggregate values across pushes.
aggregate_metrics.description:
Only applies to counters and histograms as gauges and summaries can't be
meaningfully aggregated.

keepalive.description: Configuration of HTTP server keepalive parameters.

acknowledgements.title: Controls how acknowledgements are handled by this source.
acknowledgements.description:
This setting is **deprecated** in favor of enabling `acknowledgements` at the [global][global_acks] or sink level.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the source level has **no effect** on acknowledgement behavior.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements
[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

address.title: The socket address to accept connections on.
address.description: The address _must_ include a port.

keepalive.description: Configuration of HTTP server keepalive parameters.

metadata_conflict_strategy.description: Defines the behavior for handling conflicting metric metadata.

oneOf.description: Silently ignore metadata conflicts, keeping the first metadata entry. This aligns with Prometheus/Thanos behavior.
Reject requests with conflicting metadata by returning an HTTP 400 error. This is the default to preserve backwards compatibility.

path.description: The URL path on which metric POST requests are accepted.

skip_nan_values.title: Whether to skip/discard received samples with NaN values.
skip_nan_values.description:
When enabled, any metric sample with a NaN value will be filtered out
during parsing, preventing downstream processing of invalid metrics.

endpoint_tag.title: The tag name added to each event representing the scraped instance's endpoint.
endpoint_tag.description: The tag value is the endpoint of the scraped instance.

honor_labels.title: Controls how tag conflicts are handled if the scraped source has tags to be added.
honor_labels.description:
If `true`, the new tag is not added if the scraped metric has the tag already. If `false`, the conflicting tag
is renamed by prepending `exported_` to the original name.

This matches Prometheus’ `honor_labels` configuration.

instance_tag.title: The tag name added to each event representing the scraped instance's `host:port`.
instance_tag.description: The tag value is the host and port of the scraped instance.

query.title: Custom parameters for the scrape request query string.
query.description:
One or more values for the same parameter key can be provided. The parameters provided in this option are
appended to any parameters manually provided in the `endpoints` option. This option is especially useful when
scraping the `/federate` endpoint.

scrape_interval_secs.description:
The interval between scrapes. Requests are run concurrently so if a scrape takes longer
than the interval a new scrape will be started. This can take extra resources, set the timeout
to a value lower than the scrape interval to prevent this from happening.

scrape_timeout_secs.description: The timeout for each scrape request.

acknowledgements.title: Controls how acknowledgements are handled by this source.
acknowledgements.description:
This setting is **deprecated** in favor of enabling `acknowledgements` at the [global][global_acks] or sink level.

Enabling or disabling acknowledgements at the source level has **no effect** on acknowledgement behavior.

See [End-to-end Acknowledgements][e2e_acks] for more information on how event acknowledgement is handled.

[global_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/reference/configuration/global-options/#acknowledgements
[e2e_acks]: https://vector.dev/docs/architecture/end-to-end-acknowledgements/

oneOf.description: Authentication configuration.
Basic authentication.

name.title: Basic authentication name/username.
name.description:
This can be used either for basic authentication (username/password) or JWT authentication.
When used for JWT, the value should be `token`.

token.title: Basic authentication password/token.
token.description:
This can be used either for basic authentication (username/password) or JWT authentication.
When used for JWT, the value should be the signed JWT, in the compact representation.

oneOf.description: OAuth authentication.

credentials_url.title: The credentials URL.
credentials_url.description: A data URL is also supported.

batch_size.description: Max count of messages in a batch.

oneOf.description: Dead Letter Queue policy configuration.

dead_letter_topic.description: Name of the dead letter topic where the failing messages will be sent.

max_redeliver_count.description: Maximum number of times that a message will be redelivered before being sent to the dead letter queue.

decoding.description:
Configures how events are decoded from raw bytes. Note some decoders can also determine the event output
type (log, metric, trace).

endpoint.description: The endpoint to which the Pulsar client should connect to.

framing.title: Framing configuration.
framing.description:
Framing handles how events are separated when encoded in a raw byte form, where each event is
a frame that must be prefixed, or delimited, in a way that marks where an event begins and
ends within the byte stream.

log_namespace.description: The namespace to use for logs. This overrides the global setting.

priority_level.title: The consumer's priority level.
priority_level.description:
The broker follows descending priorities. For example, 0=max-priority, 1, 2,...

In Shared subscription type, the broker first dispatches messages to the max priority level consumers if they have permits. Otherwise, the broker considers next priority level consumers.

tls.description: TLS options configuration for the Pulsar client.

ca_file.description: File path containing a list of PEM encoded certificates

verify_certificate.title: Enables certificate verification.
verify_certificate.description: Do NOT set this to `false` unless you understand the risks of not verifying the validity of certificates.

verify_hostname.title: Whether hostname verification is enabled when verify_certificate is false
verify_hostname.description: Set to true if not specified.

topics.description: The Pulsar topic names to read events from.

Set to `""` to suppress this key.

multicast_groups.title: List of IPv4 multicast groups to join on socket's binding process.
multicast_groups.description:
In order to read multicast packets, this source's listening address should be set to `0.0.0.0`.
If any other address is used (such as `127.0.0.1` or an specific interface address), the
listening interface will filter out all multicast packets received,
as their target IP would be the one of the multicast group
and it will not match the socket's bound IP.

Note that this setting will only work if the source's address
is an IPv4 address (IPv6 and systemd file descriptor as source's address are not supported
with multicast groups).

By default, `"port"` is used.

Set to `""` to suppress this key.

ack_idle_cleanup.title: Whether or not to remove channels after idling for `max_idle_time` seconds.
ack_idle_cleanup.description: A channel is idling if it is not used for sending data or querying acknowledgement statuses.

Channels can potentially idle for longer than this setting but clients should not rely on such behavior.

Minimum of `1`.

store_hec_token.title: Whether or not to forward the Splunk HEC authentication token with events.
store_hec_token.description:
If set to `true`, when incoming requests contain a Splunk HEC token, the token used is kept in the
event metadata and preferentially used if the event is sent to a Splunk HEC sink.

token.title: Optional authorization token.
token.description:
If supplied, incoming requests must supply this token in the `Authorization` header, just as a client would if
it was communicating with the Splunk HEC endpoint directly.

If _not_ supplied, the `Authorization` header is ignored and requests are not authenticated.

_metadata.deprecated_message: This option has been deprecated, use `valid_tokens` instead.

valid_tokens.title: A list of valid authorization tokens.
valid_tokens.description:
If supplied, incoming requests must supply one of these tokens in the `Authorization` header, just as a client
would if it was communicating with the Splunk HEC endpoint directly.

If _not_ supplied, the `Authorization` header is ignored and requests are not authenticated.
